element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn pitch selection and timing in practice or early games
Decision-making on when to advance bases, stealing mechanics
Positioning outfielders, throwing accuracy, cutoff management
Building optimal roster, understanding player attributes and roles
Reading opponent patterns, adapting strategy across 12 distinct teams

typical_tension_triggers: Runner on base in scoring position
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based rhythm with natural tension peaks during at-bats and base-running decisions; full game structure mirrors baseball's inning-by-inning pacing

difficulty_curve.early_game: Tutorial-friendly with basic team vs CPU opponent; players learn core batting and fielding
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Intermediate CPU teams with varying strategies; increased complexity in base-running decisions and defensive positioning
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship scenarios against stronger AI teams; mastery of all mechanics required for competitive play
notable_spikes: Transition from practice to season play with diverse opponent strategies
Late-inning high-pressure situations with runners in scoring position

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard 9-inning structure; each inning presents offense/defense alternation with escalating situational complexity

Sequential games against 12 distinct professional teams (Atlanta Amoebas, Boston Buzzards, Chicago Cyclops, New York Mercs, Philadelphia Hawks, Washington Weasels, Hawaii Islanders, Kansas City Kings, Los Angeles Lizards, Seattle Storm, Texas Tornados, Utah Stars); each team presents unique AI difficulty and playstyle
Head-to-head competitive structure allowing both players to customize teams; alternating control mimics competitive turn structure
